data.name: Product Manager II
data.jobDescription:
Overview

Are you passionate about the breadth of Microsoft’s customers - from Information Workers to IT admins - and do you want to help make Copilot an indispensable tool for every Excel user, especially on mobile?

If so, the Excel Chat team would love to talk to you.

About the Team

Excel Chat builds Copilot-powered experiences that help people understand, transform, and act on data in Excel. We partner across Excel engineering, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Office design/research, and identity/security/compliance to deliver experiences that are fast, trustworthy, and globally scalable.

We’re a collaborative, customer-obsessed team that values tight feedback loops - from customer conversations to telemetry to experimentation - and we move quickly while protecting trust.

About the Position

You will join as a Product Manager responsible for bringing agentic, intelligent experiences to Excel Mobile - experiences that don’t just answer questions, but help users complete multi-step tasks inside real workbooks.

You’ll work closely with engineering, design, and partner teams to define scenarios, ship features, and iterate based on user feedback and data. This is a strong fit for someone who loves combining crisp product thinking with hands-on execution.

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.



Responsibilities
  • Deeply understanding Excel mobile users and their workflows (quick analysis, cleanup, formula creation, charting, summarization, sharing), and translating them into clear product requirements.
  • Seeking out customer and community input whenever possible, and using feedback to guide prioritization and design decisions.
  • Partnering closely with engineering and design to ship agentic experiences that are grounded in workbook context (ranges, formulas, structured tables) and feel native on mobile.
  • Driving near-term delivery and optimization - improving task success, time-to-value, and experience quality through iteration.
  • Using telemetry and experimentation (A/B testing, flights, cohorts) to learn quickly, identify pain points, and improve outcomes.
  • Building trust into the product through transparent previews, clear “what changed” moments, and strong undo/revert patterns.
  • Collaborating across teams (Copilot, M365 platform, identity/security/compliance) to ensure the experience is reliable and enterprise-ready.
  • Communicating clearly - writing crisp PRD's, aligning stakeholders, and keeping execution unblocked.


Qualifications

Required Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's Degree AND 2+ years experience in product/service/program management or software development
    • OR equivalent experience. 
  • Product management experience shipping customer-facing features at scale.
  • Ability to work effectively in a cross-functional environment with engineering, design, and partner teams.
  • Solid product judgment and a bias for execution.
  • Comfort with telemetry, experimentation, and iterative delivery.
  • Are customer- and data-obsessed, always learning and refining features for real impact.
  • Love pushing for clarity and can explain complex systems in straightforward language.
  • Know how to balance strong opinions with being a great team player.

Other Requirements:

Ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings:

  • Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud background check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience with Copilot/LLM-powered experiences, agentic workflows, or tool-invocation patterns.
  • Mobile product experience (iOS/Android) and familiarity with performance/reliability constraints.
  • Knowledge of productivity/spreadsheet workflows and enterprise governance considerations.

This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.



Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about requesting accommodations.

blogs.title: Microsoft Sovereign Cloud adds governance, productivity and support for large AI models securely ru
blogs.description: As digital sovereignty becomes a strategic requirement, organizations are rethinking how they deploy critical infrastructure and AI capabilities under tighter regulatory expectations and higher risk conditions. Microsoft’s approach to sovereignty is grounded in enabling enterprises, public sectors and regulated industries to participate in the digital economy securely, independently and on their own terms. The Microsoft Sovereign Cloud brings together productivity, security and cloud workloads to span both... The post  Microsoft Sovereign Cloud adds governance, productivity and support for large AI models securely running even when completely disconnected   appeared first on  The Official Microsoft Blog .
